recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations and hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path are both at stable rates across 4+ consecutive cycles with zero improvement — structural hook gap (absolute-path /usr/bin/grep not intercepted) is the primary enforcement gap; recommend hook enhancement to intercept /usr/bin/grep invocations before addressing behavioral recommendations that have shown no uptake

trusted_linter_reminder_without_diffing now at 8 consecutive confirmed cycles — written mistake-pattern entry demonstrably insufficient for prevention; a PreToolUse hook that auto-runs git diff when a file-modified system-reminder is detected would address this structurally rather than behaviorally

re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read have plateaued at 3 for three consecutive cycles after declining from 5 — no further regression but no improvement; current plateau may represent a floor without a structural enforcement mechanism (e.g. hook that requires Read before Edit on first touch of a file in a unit)

deprioritize new behavioral recommendations for path_assumption_bias and declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise — both are flagged across 10+ cycles; incremental recommendation additions have produced no measurable change; structural solutions (hook enforcement, automated path resolution) should be evaluated instead
